metadata.title: Addendum to the Guideline on antiarrhythmics on atrial fibrillation and atrial flutter

rules.rule_text: The ECG is the only means to demonstrate a direct antiarrhythmic effect. Antiarrhythmics for AF must demonstrate an effect on either rhythm or rate, depending on the claimed indication.
rules.verbatim_quote: The ECG is the only means to demonstrate that a drug has a direct effect on an arrhythmia. Therefore, antiarrhythmics intended for AF by definition need to demonstrate an effect either on the rhythm or rate, depending on the claimed indication

rules.rule_text: For restoration of sinus rhythm indication, the primary endpoint must be termination of AF/AFL ('successful conversion') as measured during continuous ECG monitoring.
rules.verbatim_quote: If the claimed indication is restoration of sinus rhythm by pharmacological cardioversion, the primary endpoint will be termination of the AF/AFL ('successful conversion') as measured during continuous ECG monitoring.

rules.rule_text: Successful conversion must require patients to remain in sinus rhythm for a clinically relevant period of time. The definition of success rates must be defined in the protocol as there is no widely accepted standard.
rules.verbatim_quote: From a clinical perspective, it is necessary to ensure that patients remain in sinus rhythm for a period of time that is deemed relevant in the clinical context where the study is conducted. ... As there is no widely accepted definition for success rates, these should be defined in the protocol.

rules.rule_text: Cardioversion studies should target patients with recent-onset AF, as patients with longer-lasting episodes (>7 days) are more difficult to cardiovert with drugs alone.
rules.verbatim_quote: Usually, cardioversion studies are targeted at patients with recent-onset AF as patients with longer-lasting AF episodes (> 7 days) are more difficult to cardiovert with drugs alone.

rules.rule_text: Time to recurrence and recurrence rates must be monitored. Time limit for assessment of the primary endpoint must be defined in the protocol.
rules.verbatim_quote: Time to recurrence and recurrence rates should also be monitored in order to assess its clinical relevance. The time limit for assessment of the primary endpoint should be defined, as a too short time window may limit the possibility to observe the spontaneous cardioversion rate.

rules.rule_text: For maintenance of sinus rhythm after cardioversion in persistent/permanent AF/AFL, time to first documented recurrence from cardioversion or baseline is a clinically meaningful primary endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: If the claimed indication is (1) maintenance of sinus rhythm after cardioversion in patients with persistent or permanent AF/AFL, time to first documented recurrence of the AF from cardioversion or baseline is a clinically meaningful primary endpoint.

rules.rule_text: For prevention of recurrence of paroxysmal AF, suitable primary outcomes may consist of time to recurrence of AF or time in AF. Number of episodes should be a secondary endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: If (2) prevention of recurrence of paroxysmal AF is the primary target, the primary outcome is more difficult to define. Suitable primary outcomes may consist of time to recurrence of AF or time in AF. In case of recurrence the number of such episodes should be taken into account as secondary endpoint.

rules.rule_text: Any arrhythmia with ECG characteristics of AF/AFL lasting longer than 30 seconds must be reported as recurrent AF/AFL.
rules.verbatim_quote: Any arrhythmia that has ECG characteristics of AF/AFL lasting longer than 30s should be reported as recurrent AF/AFL.

rules.rule_text: Systematic ECG recordings are always required and must be scheduled regularly, particularly during the first week after cardioversion, as symptom-based ECG recordings will miss about half of all AF/AFL episodes.
rules.verbatim_quote: Systematic ECG recordings are always required, and should be scheduled regularly, in particular during the first week after cardioversion, as ECG recordings by symptoms will miss about half of all AF/AFL episodes.

rules.rule_text: Arrhythmia free-survival rate should be measured as a secondary endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: Arrhythmia free-survival rate should be measured as a secondary endpoint.

rules.rule_text: Study design must account for early recurrences of AF that are common after cardioversion. Initiation of drug therapy to achieve therapeutic levels at the time of cardioversion is often more suitable than a blanking period.
rules.verbatim_quote: Study design should take into consideration that early recurrences of AF are common after cardioversion. Often, initiation of drug therapy to achieve therapeutic drug levels at the time of cardioversion is more suitable than a 'blanking period' immediately after cardioversion.

rules.rule_text: For rate control in chronic AF/AFL, effects on mean resting heart rate (12-lead ECG) and heart rate during moderate exercise testing are relevant efficacy measures. 24-hour Holter monitoring can be used as an alternative.
rules.verbatim_quote: If the claimed indication is rate control in patients with chronic AF/AFL, effects on mean resting heart rate as measured by 12 lead ECG and heart rate during moderate exercise testing are relevant measures of efficacy. ... Alternatively, 24-hour Holter monitoring can be used.

rules.rule_text: 24-hour Holter monitoring is also useful to check for excessive bradycardias and must be performed at baseline and when steady state concentrations have been achieved.
rules.verbatim_quote: 24-hour Holter monitoring is also useful to check for excessive bradycardias. Measurements should be performed at baseline, and when steady state concentrations have been achieved.

rules.rule_text: Criteria used to define adequate rate control must be specified in the protocol, considering rate at rest and during a level of activity achievable by the individual patient.
rules.verbatim_quote: Criteria used to define adequate control should be mentioned in the protocol, considering the rate at rest and/or during a level of activity that can typically be achieved by the individual patient.

rules.rule_text: Clinical benefit in terms of symptomatic improvement and/or morbidity and mortality must be assessed before marketing authorization can be obtained, regardless of whether the primary endpoint is rhythm or rate control.
rules.verbatim_quote: The primary efficacy endpoint in most trials will focus on the arrhythmia per se, be it rhythm or rate control, but clinical benefit in terms of symptomatic improvement and/or morbidity and mortality needs to be assessed before a marketing authorisation can be obtained.

rules.rule_text: Anticoagulation treatment must be optimized before patients are included in the study. This must be defined in the protocol and follow current clinical practice guidelines.
rules.verbatim_quote: It should be recognised that incorrect anticoagulation increases morbidity and mortality and utmost care should be taken to optimise anticoagulation treatment before patients are included in the study. This should be defined in the protocol and should follow the present recommendations in the clinical practice guidelines.

rules.rule_text: For reduction of cardiovascular events indication, the preferred approach is a primary composite endpoint comprising cardiovascular death and disease-related morbidity (new onset/worsening heart failure, stroke, MI).
rules.verbatim_quote: If the claimed indication is reduction of cardiovascular events including mortality the most favoured approach would be to choose one primary composite endpoint comprising of cardiovascular death and disease-related morbidity, such as new onset or worsening heart failure, stroke, and/or myocardial infarction.

rules.rule_text: All deaths must be monitored on an intention-to-treat basis from randomization and must be classified. AF/AFL-related death should not substitute 'total' cardiovascular death.
rules.verbatim_quote: All deaths need to be monitored in any trial on an 'intention-to-treat' basis from the time of randomisation and should be classified. AF/AFL-related death should not substitute 'total' cardiovascular death as an outcome parameter, because AF/AFL-related death will be difficult to assess.

rules.rule_text: All strokes (ischaemic and haemorrhagic) and systemic events must be recorded separately and evaluated using best possible methods including MRI/CT brain imaging, assessing anticoagulation intensity, severity of stroke, and neurological end results.
rules.verbatim_quote: All strokes (ischaemic and haemorrhagic) and systemic events should be recorded separately and be evaluated using the best possible methods (including MRI/CT brain imaging, assessing intensity of anticoagulation, severity of stroke, and neurological end results).

rules.rule_text: All events (CV death, stroke, MI, heart failure) must be adjudicated by an independent committee.
rules.verbatim_quote: All events should be adjudicated by an independent committee.

rules.rule_text: LV function must be measured at baseline and during follow-up by echocardiography (LV volumes and ejection fraction). A standardized submaximal exercise test should supplement this.
rules.source_section: 5.4 Left ventricular function and exercise testing
rules.verbatim_quote: Global LV function should be measured at baseline by echocardiography or another validated method and during follow-up by measuring LV volumes as well as ejection fraction. This can be supplemented by a standardized submaximal exercise test as a general measure of cardiac performance.

rules.rule_text: Mortality and morbidity data must always be reported regardless of the clinical claim. Hospitalizations for both cardiovascular and non-cardiac causes must be monitored closely.
rules.verbatim_quote: Mortality and morbidity data must always be reported whatever the clinical claim. Hospitalisations both for cardiovascular adverse events and for non-cardiac causes should be monitored closely.

rules.rule_text: When drugs target cardiac ion channels or patients with grade II-IV heart failure are the target population, controlled total and cardiovascular studies are required. If not completed at authorization, a clear contraindication in heart failure patients will ensue.
rules.verbatim_quote: This is especially the case when drugs are targeting cardiac ion channels and when patients with grade II - IV heart failure are the target population in the trial. ... If such studies are not completed by the time of authorisation, a clear contraindication of use of the drug in patients with heart failure will ensue.

rules.rule_text: Cardiovascular function monitoring must include automaticity, AV conduction, QT shortening and prolongation. Occurrence of arrhythmias (sick sinus syndrome, bradycardia, AV block, torsade de pointes, new ventricular arrhythmias) must be carefully documented.
rules.verbatim_quote: Effects on cardiovascular function, in particular on automaticity, AV conduction and QT shortening and prolongation should be monitored. Occurrence of arrhythmias, in particular sick sinus syndrome or another cause of bradycardia, atrioventricular block and torsade de pointes and other (new) ventricular arrhythmias, should be carefully documented.

rules.rule_text: Any arrhythmia that might constitute a pro-arrhythmic event must be reported as an adverse event. Patients at risk (structural heart disease, heart failure) must be observed for exaggerated pharmacological response.
rules.verbatim_quote: Any arrhythmia that might constitute a pro-arrhythmic event must be reported as an adverse event. Patients at risk, in particular patients with structural heart disease and/or heart failure should be observed for any exaggerated pharmacological response.

rules.rule_text: Special attention must be paid to PK/PD interactions with other cardiovascular drugs affecting heart rate, automaticity, AV conduction, QT prolongation, and with anticoagulants.
rules.verbatim_quote: Special attention should be paid to pharmacokinetic and/or pharmacodynamic interactions with other cardiovascular drugs affecting heart rate, automaticity, AV conduction, QT prolongation, and with antithrombotics, in particular anticoagulants.

rules.rule_text: Patients must be selected according to arrhythmia pattern (first detected, paroxysmal, persistent, permanent). A sufficient percentage of patients with impaired LVEF (<35%) must be included. Both genders and elderly must be adequately represented.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients should be selected according to the arrhythmia pattern of AF or AFL (first detected, paroxysmal, persistent, permanent). ... A sufficient percentage of patients with impaired LVEF (<35%) should be included in clinical trials when these patients reflect the true target population. ... An adequate number of elderly patients should be included in the clinical trials and both genders should be represented adequately.

rules.rule_text: Both placebo-controlled and actively controlled double-blind studies should be considered. Active-controlled studies are necessary from a regulatory perspective when the benefit/risk balance appears altered compared to the comparator.
rules.verbatim_quote: Both placebo-controlled and actively controlled double-blind studies should be considered. Active controlled studies are feasible and, in general, necessary from a regulatory perspective in particular when the balance between benefit and risk appears to be altered compared to the comparator.
rules.exceptions: For paroxysmal AF/AFL with brief or minimally symptomatic recurrences, placebo use can be justified
